name: Risk
description:
Risk is a turn-based strategy game played on a world map divided into territories. The core gameplay revolves around players strategically deploying armies to their territories and attacking adjacent territories to conquer them. The objective is world domination, achieved by eliminating all opposing players. Each player starts with a set number of armies and territories, and during their turn, they can reinforce their territories, attack enemy territories, and fortify their borders. 

Attacks are resolved through dice rolls, with the attacker and defender each rolling dice to determine casualties. The number of dice rolled depends on the number of armies present in the attacking and defending territories. Combat is deterministic based on these dice rolls. Territory cards can be earned and traded in for reinforcements at the beginning of a player's turn. Card sets provide bonus armies based on the type of territories depicted on the cards. The game continues until one player controls all territories on the map, or until a pre-defined turn limit is reached, potentially resulting in a stalemate scenario. The game handles multiple AI opponents of varying difficulty levels. The game involves an initial army deployment phase followed by the main turn-based gameplay loop involving reinforcement, attack and fortification stages.

controls.w: Select/Highlight the next territory that can be acted upon based on the current turn state (e.g. territory ready to reinforce, territory from which to attack, territory to which to fortify)
controls.s: Select/Highlight the previous territory that can be acted upon based on the current turn state (e.g. territory ready to reinforce, territory from which to attack, territory to which to fortify)
controls.a: Decrease the number of armies selected for deployment/attack/fortification
controls.d: Increase the number of armies selected for deployment/attack/fortification
controls.space: Confirm selection / Execute action (e.g. deploy armies, initiate attack, confirm fortification)
controls.shift: Hold to zoom out on the map; release to return to normal zoom level
controls.q: Cycle through available actions for a selected territory (Reinforce -> Attack -> Fortify)
controls.z: Toggle display of territory ownership (highlighting territories based on player color)
controls.x: View player statistics (armies remaining, territories controlled, cards held)
